name: Varpulis
description: Varpulis, a relatively obscure yet significant figure in Baltic mythology, is primarily associated with the sound of the bell and, more broadly, with communication, warnings, and the transition between worlds. While not as prominent as Perkūnas or Laima, Varpulis occupies a unique niche, representing the audible signals that guide and protect individuals. Some accounts suggest Varpulis acted as a messenger god, delivering news from the divine realm to mortals, often through the ringing of a bell. This role connected him to both the earthly and spiritual planes, making him a crucial intermediary. The ringing of Varpulis' bell could signify danger, calling people to action, or announce important events, such as births, deaths, or victories. His power resided not in physical strength or dominion over natural phenomena, but in the intangible yet potent influence of sound. He was invoked for protection against ill omens and to ensure clear communication with the gods. In some later interpretations, Varpulis became linked to the church bell, further cementing his association with announcements and spiritual guidance. Though lacking elaborate myths compared to major deities, Varpulis' presence is felt in the traditions and folklore surrounding bells and their significance in everyday life. His influence underscores the Baltic people's deep respect for the power of sound and its ability to shape perceptions and influence destinies. He is the guardian of announcements and the protector from miscommunication.
subtitle: Guardian of Bells and Announcements

keyMyths.title: Varpulis and the Lost Travelers
keyMyths.description: Lost travelers in a dense forest hear the faint ringing of Varpulis' bell. Following the sound, they are guided to safety and a village where they are welcomed and given shelter.

keyMyths.title: The Warning Bell
keyMyths.description: A village is about to be attacked by raiders, but Varpulis rings his bell, alerting the villagers. They manage to defend themselves and thwart the attack, crediting Varpulis with their survival.

keyMyths.title: Varpulis's Message to the Gods
keyMyths.description: When prayers were not being heard, a mortal rang Varpulis' bell with pure intention. The sound carried the prayer to the gods, who then answered the mortal's plea, showing Varpulis's power as a messenger.

sources.author: Gintaras Beresnevičius
sources.description: Comprehensive dictionary of Baltic religion and mythology.
sources.text: Comprehensive dictionary of Baltic religion and mythology.
